sglang.title: Batch EAGLE draft/draft-extend replay memcpys via grouped foreach copy

Summary

Batch the per-field device copies in EAGLEDraftCudaGraphRunner.replay() and EAGLEDraftExtendCudaGraphRunner.replay() into a single _grouped_foreach_copy_ call (one torch._foreach_copy_ per dtype pair) instead of many individual copy_() launches, cutting kernel-launch overhead on the replay

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Cherry-pick of #28371 (commit 093908d, already on main) onto sglang-miles.
What it fixes
In CUDA-graph mode the chunked-SGMV (csgmv) LoRA kernels baked the grid size (batch_info.bs) and the num_segments scalar into the captured graph at capture time. On replay, the real batch's segment count differs

In get_w8a8_block_fp8_configs, when BLOCK_SIZE_K < block_k, it clamps BLOCK_SIZE_K to block_k, which may cause the error (L40): triton.runtime.errors.OutOfResources: out of resource: shared memory, Required: 148480, Hardware limit: 101376.
In fact, on CUDA, when block_k % BLOCK_SIZE_K ==

sglang.author: avjves
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Currently, in SP, only the image is sharded and the text is replicated on all GPUs. While this saves some comms time by not needing to transmit the replicated text in the all2all calls, it also balloons the GEMMs. As the entire text is kept, each GPU runs the full text through all the ope

The decode-path append in process_batch_result_decode treats logits_output.hidden_states as [bs, hidden_dim], but in spec V2 it is strided [bs * speculative_num_draft_tokens, hidden_dim]. Row i is the bonus position of req i, not its accepted-tokens slice — so meta_info["hidden

The trtllm_mha backend currently requires seq_lens on the host (CPU): it sizes its attention metadata — specifically the page-table width — from max(seq_lens), so it sets needs_cpu_seq_lens=True and the scheduler copies seq_lens back to the host every step.

Purpose
Add Python-supervised Rust frontend support for DP LB modes where each Rust frontend owns an explicit engine rank range. This covers external DP LB with one frontend per DP rank and hybrid DP LB with one frontend per node managing multiple local DP r

vllm.title: [KV Connector][Offloading] Avoid blocking the engine to flush offloads on idle
vllm.author: Etelis
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
When all tracked requests finish, OffloadingConnectorScheduler.build_connector_meta
flushed every in-flight job through a blocking worker.wait() (added in #42611). That
wait runs on the engine-core thread, so a request arriving while
it runs cannot be scheduled until the offload transfers complete.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fixes MiniCPM-O resampler device placement to avoid tensor device mismatch
vllm.author: j9smith
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes #42322. MiniCPM-O 2.6 crashes on startup with Expected all tensors to be on the same device.
This is caused by Resampler2_5._set_2d_pos_cache creating the pos_embed buffer on CPU during init (found in minicpmv.py). Typically, it would be moved to GPU via MiniCPMVBaseModel._ensure_resam

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixes #45834.
After #45553 the Gemma4 tool chat template inserts <|channel>thought\n into
the generation prompt when continuing a turn after a tool response with
enable_thinking=True. The prompt token IDs therefore end inside an open
reasoning channel (a <|channel> start with no matching <ch

vllm.title: [Bugfix][test] Use Salesforce/wikitext for ppl tests
vllm.author: wentian-byte
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
datasets >= 5.0 + huggingface_hub >= 1.19 no longer accept the legacy single-segment id 'wikitext' (HfUriError: 'Repository id must be namespace/name'). The dataset moved to 'Salesforce/wikitext'.
Without this fix, all PPL tests in tests/models/language/generation_ppl_test/ fail at load_dataset() be

vllm.title: fix(security): enforce audio decode duration limit in chat completions path
vllm.author: jperezdealgaba
vllm.bodyExcerpt: AudioMediaIO.load_bytes() and load_file() called load_audio() without max_duration_s, bypassing the decompression-bomb guard for audio loaded via /v1/chat/completions (input_audio content parts). Wire VLLM_MAX_AUDIO_DECODE_DURATION_S into both methods to enforce the same limit that already protects

vllm.bodyExcerpt:

On ROCm with AITER enabled, FP8-quantized attention projections produced by
Quark silently fall back to the generic torch._scaled_mm
(hipBLASLt/Tensile Cijk_*_F8BS_*) path instead of AITER's faster pre-shuffled
GEMM (gemm_a8w8_bpreshuffle).
The root cause is a bug in vLLM's Quark integration

The is_mla_region check in base_worker.py only recognizes MLAAttentionSpec, but DSV4's compressor layers are SlidingWindowMLASpec.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Complete one-shot fused all-reduce PDL at end to avoid NaN
vllm.author: alexeldeib
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The FlashInfer one-shot Lamport all-reduce signals programmatic-dependent- launch (PDL) completion before committing its output buffer when trigger_completion_at_end=False, so the next PDL-launched kernel reads the uninitialized buffer and produces NaN. The fused AR+RMSNorm pass set the flag

vllm.title: [Rust Frontend][Perf] O(n) argument scan in tool parser
vllm.author: BugenZhao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Previously in many tool parsers, when we scan the argument body, we used a trivial take_until. This handles partial input correctly, but not that efficiently: if it returns Incomplete, we do not persist any scan state, so the next scan still starts from the beginning of the buffered input. E

The Rust lowering path tokenizes bad_words into _bad_words_token_ids, but previously did not

vllm.title: [CPUOffloading] Guard CPU eviction check
vllm.author: varun-sundar-rabindranath
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Eviction can be expensive when the CPU cache is big and full. The eviction logic walks all the blocks to check if there is a candidate.
The PR adds logic to track the idle cache blocks based on the load and store requests and escapes the check if the eviction is guaranteed to fail.
Compariso

vllm.title: [SimpleCPUOffloadConnector]: Add support for reset_cache()
vllm.author: jonathanc-n
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
This pull request adds reset_cache() functionality by clearing scheduler side state + resetting all prefix cache
During Scheduler.reset_prefix_cache() it runs kv_cache_manager.reset_prefix_cache() which will fail for in-flight DMA transfers as there are still ref_cnt > 0 blocks in the pool.
